text:
Market today: Mixed signals everywhere — volatility indicators are stuck in a tug-of-war between bulls and bears. Memory chip cost pressures are a real headwind for your chip-heavy book, while the AI efficiency shift (less compute, not more) is a quiet drag on names that rode the tokenmaxxing wave.

Your holdings: PLTR is your sharpest pain point at -17.56% — on margin, that stings hard and it's flagged SELL, not just trim. AVGO is your largest position and down -13.18%; it's already on TRIM watch and chip cost pressure hits it directly. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — on margin, a fast gap-down here could hurt fast and leave you little room to react before your $150 stop.

Watch: (1) Any follow-through on memory chip cost headlines — AVGO and SMH move with that narrative. (2) PLTR's price action relative to your $85 stop — at $107 it has room, but the SELL flag means the trend is your enemy.

Bottom line: Your two biggest fires are PLTR and AVGO — on margin, sitting in losers this size is a choice, not a default.
